name: Bob Thiele
abstract: Robert Thiele (July 27, 1922 – January 30, 1996) was an American record producer who worked on numerous classic jazz albums and record labels.

about_quotes.speaker: Lonnie Liston Smith
about_quotes.evidence: So when I was with Miles Bob Thiele called me and said, “Lonnie, people are talking about you all over the world, so it’s time for you to do your own record.” ... So we did the record and I called it “Astral Traveling” ... Bob Thiele produced all those records by John Coltrane. And then he told me a
about_quotes.doc_title: Lonnie Liston Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 9, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: I was approached – my old friend Stanley Dance... he had gotten himself involved with Bob Thiele... He wanted to restart this magazine called Jazz... Bob, who had been married a number of times... I was there to groom Pauline... Bob was then producing for Impulse!... My editorial job at Jazz was unp
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: And then I was recording with Bob Thiele, he said, “This is a good time for you to do your own recordings”. He had been recording John’s quartet. He was the first guy to encourage me to record on my own, Bob Thiele, he was a nice guy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Scott
about_quotes.evidence: I did two albums for Bob Thiele, Impulse Records... my very first album was for Bob Thiele of Impulse... I got to see Bob Thiele in his office at Impulse Records at the time, and it was time to do another, a second album.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Scott interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2022 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: Bob Thiele had this idea for a record that’s really a good record, called Shades of Bix. It’s a lot of Bix tunes, but done in Jimmy’s style. And some wonderful bands. I’ve got these records upstairs. They have people like Coleman Hawkins on them and . . . can’t remember the personnel.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Arthur "Monk" Hazel
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...It's a band that Bob Thiel put together in New York to kind of spoof, I guess, old-time... [INTERVIEWEE] That's the way most of the hotel bands in the old days... I was surprised when you told me who it was that was on there.
